q: My best-selling SKU costs $20 to make.
a:
That's a helpful starting point! To give you a precise margin and market analysis, could you please tell me the name of this best-selling SKU?
Once I have the name, I can look up its current price in your store and compare it against the $20 cost you provided.

q: I want a 50% margin on that product. What should I price it at?
a:
To achieve a 50% gross margin on a product that costs $20 to make, you should price it at $40.00.
Calculation:
$20.00 / (1 - 0.50) = $40.00

Calculation:
$28.00 / (1 - 0.50) = $56.00

q: Which of my two products has better margin potential?
a:
Based on the numbers provided, Scenario 1 ($28 cost, $56 price) has better margin potential on paper for two reasons:

Higher Efficiency: A 50% margin gives you more "room" to cover ads, overhead, and shipping than a 40% margin.
Higher Absolute Profit: You earn $28 per unit compared to $10 per unit
